message.group_desc:
Yeti’s Grind Coffee is located inside Solaris, placing it in the very center of Vail Village steps from Gondola One, Bridge Street, and nearby lodging. Its ground-level location within Solaris makes it an easy walk for skiers, shoppers, and visitors moving through the village core.

The café specializes in coffee and espresso drinks with a grab-and-go service style designed for efficiency. The menu focuses on crafted coffee beverages, teas, and light breakfast items, making it a practical stop for fueling up before skiing or starting a day in the village.

The atmosphere at Yeti’s Grind Coffee is casual and fast-paced, especially during morning hours. Seating is limited, reinforcing its role as a quick coffee stop rather than a long sit-down café, while still offering a comfortable place for short breaks.

Yeti’s Grind Coffee is ideal for skiers heading to the lifts, visitors staying in Vail Village, locals grabbing coffee on the go, and anyone looking for a reliable caffeine stop in a prime village location.

Why Guests Choose Yeti’s Grind Coffee

  • Located inside Solaris in Vail Village

  • Steps from Gondola One and Bridge Street

  • Quick-service coffee and espresso drinks

  • Ideal for pre-ski mornings and daytime breaks

  • Highly walkable village-core location
message.post_location: 2271 N Frontage Rd W Ste B Vail, CO 81657

message.group_desc:
Yoshimi Pop-Up is a seasonal dining concept located inside the Grand Hyatt Vail, offering a limited-time restaurant experience within a resort setting just west of Vail Village. Its location provides convenient access for Grand Hyatt guests while remaining reachable by car or shuttle from other parts of Vail.

The menu at Yoshimi Pop-Up centers on Japanese-inspired cuisine, typically featuring sushi, sashimi, and carefully prepared small plates. Menu offerings may change throughout the season, reflecting the pop-up format and ingredient availability.

The dining atmosphere is intimate and contemporary, with a quieter, more focused setting compared to larger resort restaurants. Seating is generally limited, making the experience more personal and well suited for relaxed, evening dining.

Yoshimi Pop-Up is best suited for couples, small groups, and visitors staying at or near the Grand Hyatt Vail who are interested in a seasonal, Japanese-inspired dining option without traveling into the busier core of Vail Village.

Why Guests Choose Yoshimi Pop-Up

  • Located inside the Grand Hyatt Vail

  • Seasonal pop-up dining concept

  • Japanese-inspired menu with sushi and small plates

  • Intimate, modern dining atmosphere

  • Convenient for resort guests and nearby lodging

message.group_desc:
Zema Lingerie Vail is a specialty lingerie boutique located in the heart of Vail Village, within easy walking distance of shops, restaurants, and village lodging. Its central location makes it a convenient stop for visitors exploring the village on foot as well as locals shopping downtown.

The store focuses on high-quality lingerie, sleepwear, and intimate apparel, offering thoughtfully selected pieces designed for comfort, fit, and everyday wear. Shoppers will find a curated assortment that balances style with practicality, suitable for a range of body types and preferences.

Zema Lingerie Vail provides a calm, boutique-style shopping experience with personalized assistance, allowing customers to browse in a relaxed and discreet setting. The atmosphere is refined yet approachable, appealing to shoppers who value attentive service and well-chosen collections.

This store is ideal for women looking for everyday essentials, special-occasion pieces, or thoughtful gifts. Zema Lingerie Vail serves both visitors and locals seeking a dedicated lingerie boutique in Vail Village rather than a general apparel store.

Why Shoppers Choose Zema Lingerie Vail

  • Central location in Vail Village

  • Specialty focus on lingerie and intimate apparel

  • Boutique shopping experience with personal service

  • Suitable for everyday wear and gift shopping

  • Easy walk from dining, lodging, and village attractions

message.group_desc: SpecialtiesThe Westside Cafe in Vail, Colorado specializes in casual American comfort food. We serve breakfast all day long and serve lunch starting at 11:30am and dinner at 4pm. We welcome families and have an interactive kids menu offering healthy dining choices for the little ones. We have a full coffee bar and a full liquor bar with 4 TV's. We are recognized as Vail's best catering company and can accomodate groups up to 120 people in house. We cater for some of the top companies in the Vail valley, many of whom are repeat clients.HistoryEstablished in 2002.The Westside Cafe was started by 3 Vail locals. They all met while working at Sweet Basil in Vail Village. They were tired of steering someone else's ship, so they decided to build their own. Hence, the birth of The Westside Cafe. It has gone through numerous changes over the years, always striving to get better and better. Currently, The Westside serves a casual breakfast, lunch and dinner and does a ton of catering at all times of the day, at all locations, including their own! If you have any thoughts of improvement or otherwise, please, don't hesitate to speak up. Most conveniently, our e-mail is westsidecafe@comcast.net and is checked daily. www.WestsideCafe.netMeet the Business OwnerRyan, Mike and Steve W.Business OwnerOne of the greatest achievements of the Westside Cafe is the friendship amongst its 3 partners. They started out as friends in 2002 and consider their friendship stronger now than on day one. They focus on putting the "fun" into their work and always find room to hit the town outside of work. Join them if you ever see them out on the town, it'll be a fun experience, guaranteed.
message.post_location: 2211 N Frontage Rd W Ste 5 Vail, CO 81657

message.group_desc:
Westwind At Vail is a condo-style property offering comfortable accommodations in a convenient West Vail location. Designed for travelers who want more space and flexibility, this property works well for families, ski groups, and longer stays in the Vail Valley.

Located in West Vail, Westwind At Vail provides easy access to Vail Ski Resort, with nearby shuttle stops connecting guests to Vail Village and Lionshead. Its location offers a quieter residential feel while still keeping skiing, dining, and shopping within easy reach.

Accommodations include condo-style units with full kitchens and separate living areas, making it easy for guests to cook meals, relax after a day outdoors, and settle in for multi-night stays. The property’s layout and amenities appeal to travelers who prefer a home-style setup over traditional hotel rooms.

For those comparing Vail lodging options and looking for value, space, and a quieter setting outside the village core, Westwind At Vail is a practical choice year-round.

Why Guests Choose Westwind At Vail

• Quiet West Vail location with easy shuttle access
• Condo-style accommodations with full kitchens
• More space for families and ski groups
• Convenient access to Vail Ski Resort and village areas
• Well suited for extended stays

message.group_desc:
The Wren at Vail is a centrally located hotel offering comfortable, spacious accommodations with easy access to the best of Vail. Ideal for couples, families, and outdoor enthusiasts, this property blends a relaxed mountain atmosphere with a highly walkable, village-adjacent location.

Located near Vail Village and Lionshead, The Wren at Vail provides quick access to Vail Ski Resort, dining, shopping, and après-ski, while still offering a quieter setting just outside the busiest village core. It’s a convenient home base for ski trips, summer adventures, and longer stays in the Vail Valley.

Guests appreciate the spacious rooms and suites, many featuring kitchenettes or full kitchens, making it easy to settle in and enjoy added flexibility during your stay. After a day on the slopes or exploring town, relax and recharge with the hotel’s outdoor amenities.

Why Guests Choose The Wren at Vail

• Walkable location near Vail Village and Lionshead
• Suites with kitchenettes or full kitchens
• Heated outdoor pool and hot tub
• Easy access to Vail Ski Resort and the Eagle Bahn Gondola
• Great for couples, families, ski trips, and extended stays

With its excellent location, practical amenities, and laid-back alpine feel, The Wren at Vail is a solid choice for travelers looking for comfort, convenience, and easy access to everything Vail has to offer—year-round.

message.group_desc: Specialties: Established in 1977, Vista Bahn Ski Rentals is one of the last owner-operated ski shops in Vail. The Gros family -- Joël, Jane, Sacha and Dominique -- have lived the ski life for over 40 years and are proud of their ski heritage in Vail, Colorado. Vista Bahn Ski Rentals is the Skier's Ski Shop. Our unique location next to Vail Ski School and Gondola One at Vail Mountain means you can ski right to our door! Our knowledgeable and friendly staff provides the highest level of service to our customers. We not only offer the highest quality ski rentals, but we provide quick repairs, ski tuning and advice about boots and gear right on the spot. Our other convenient services include overnight precision tuning of your skis and lots of ski storage including full size ski lockers, baskets and coin-operated lockers. Established in 1977.  Vista Bahn Ski Rentals is one of the last owner-operated ski shops in Vail, Colorado. The Gros family -- Joël, Jane, Sacha and Dominique -- have lived and skied in Vail for over 30 years and are proud of their ski heritage in Vail, Colorado. Vista Bahn Ski Rentals is the Skier's Ski Shop. Our unique location next to Vail Ski School and Gondola One on Vail Mountain means you can pick up your rental equipment and ski right to the lift!

message.group_desc: Specialties: Goat Yoga Classes, memorable Horseback Rides, and a Saloon with the Happiest Hour in town. The Goats run wild and the kids play in the sand. Located one mile from downtown Vail, CO. The views of Vail Mountain from our location are spectacular!  Enjoy an exclusive opportunity to ride in the peaceful forest on beginner friendly to advanced gentle horses, that like to trot, if you so desire. Goat Yoga classes are held most days. The saloon is open M-Saturday, noon to 6pm with cold brew and wine. Enjoy a free game of corn hole or washers and redneck bowling. Established in 1976.  Roger T started Spraddle Creek Ranch when Vail was just a blip on the screen.  Later Marc W bought it and ran it for 21 years.  During that time, the ranch was moved from its original location on USFS land, further up the mountain.  The ranch base sits at 8400 ft.  From there, the rides will steadily climb and gently wind back down to the ranch.  In 2003, the ranch was transferred to Kym M.  The whole family takes part in keeping the operation running smoothly.  We are here to serve you, keep you safe, and show you the best time possible.

message.group_desc:
Vail's Mountain Haus is a centrally located hotel offering comfortable accommodations with convenient access to both Vail Village and Lionshead. Positioned along Gore Creek, this property appeals to travelers who want a balance of walkable access, scenic surroundings, and a relaxed mountain atmosphere.

Located near Lionshead and a short walk to Vail Village, Vail's Mountain Haus provides easy access to Vail Ski Resort, the Eagle Bahn Gondola, dining, shopping, and après-ski. Its location works well for guests who want to be close to the slopes while still enjoying a quieter setting near the creek.

Accommodations range from traditional hotel rooms to larger suites and condos, some with kitchenettes or full kitchens. This variety makes the property suitable for couples, families, and longer stays, offering flexibility depending on travel style and group size.

With its central location, practical amenities, and mix of room options, Vail's Mountain Haus is a solid choice for travelers comparing Vail hotels and looking for comfort, convenience, and year-round access to mountain activities.

Why Guests Choose Vail's Mountain Haus

• Central location between Lionshead and Vail Village
• Close access to Vail Ski Resort and Eagle Bahn Gondola
• Mix of hotel rooms, suites, and condo-style accommodations
• Pool, hot tub, and fitness amenities on-site
• Well suited for couples, families, and extended stays

message.group_desc:
Vail Racquet Club Mountain Resort is a condo-style resort offering spacious accommodations in a quiet East Vail setting. Surrounded by mountain views and natural open space, this property is well suited for travelers who prefer a relaxed atmosphere with room to spread out while still maintaining access to Vail’s main attractions.

Located in East Vail, the resort provides convenient access to Vail Ski Resort via shuttle or a short drive to Vail Village and Lionshead. Its location appeals to guests who want a peaceful base away from village crowds while remaining connected to skiing, dining, and shopping.

Accommodations include a range of condo-style units, many featuring full kitchens, separate living areas, and private balconies. The resort’s layout and amenities make it a popular choice for families, couples, and extended stays, especially for guests who value space and on-site recreation.

For travelers comparing Vail lodging options and looking for a resort-style property with a quieter setting, practical amenities, and easy mountain access, Vail Racquet Club Mountain Resort is a dependable year-round option.

Why Guests Choose Vail Racquet Club Mountain Resort

• Quiet East Vail location surrounded by nature
• Condo-style accommodations with full kitchens
• Shuttle access to Vail Ski Resort and Vail Village
• On-site recreational amenities and open space
• Well suited for families, couples, and longer stays

message.group_desc:
Vail Run Resort is a centrally located condominium-style property offering spacious accommodations just minutes from Vail Village. Positioned along the free Town of Vail shuttle route, the resort provides convenient access to Vail Ski Resort, dining, shopping, and year-round mountain activities while maintaining a quieter residential feel.

Accommodations at Vail Run Resort range from studio units to multi-bedroom condos, many featuring full kitchens, dining areas, and private balconies. These layouts are well suited for families, groups, and travelers planning longer stays who prefer the flexibility of in-room cooking and extra living space.

On-site amenities include indoor and outdoor pools, hot tubs, a fitness center, and ski storage, making the property practical for both winter ski trips and summer mountain vacations. Covered parking and elevator access add to the overall convenience for guests arriving by car.

Vail Run Resort appeals to travelers seeking a balance between proximity to the village and a more relaxed, residential environment. Its combination of space, amenities, and shuttle access makes it a reliable option for value-conscious guests comparing condo-style lodging in Vail.

Why Guests Choose Vail Run Resort

  • Free shuttle access to Vail Village and Vail Ski Resort

  • Condo-style accommodations with kitchens

  • Indoor and outdoor pools with hot tubs

  • Suitable for families, groups, and extended stays

  • Quieter setting just outside the village core

We opened our doors in 2005 with a small group of skiers who dreamed of sharing our passion for the sport and the mountain lifestyle.We offer the finest, cutting edge equipment and a clubhouse atmosphere. message.group_desc:
Vail 21 Condominiums offers condo-style accommodations in a convenient Lionshead location, providing easy access to skiing, dining, and village amenities. This property is well suited for travelers who want extra space and the flexibility of a condo while staying close to the action.

Located in Lionshead, Vail 21 Condominiums is within walking distance of Vail Ski Resort and the Eagle Bahn Gondola. Guests can quickly reach the slopes, restaurants, shops, and après-ski, making it a practical base for both winter ski trips and summer visits.

Accommodations include one- and two-bedroom condo units with full kitchens and separate living areas. These layouts are ideal for families, couples traveling together, and guests planning longer stays who prefer the convenience of in-room cooking and added space.

For travelers comparing Vail lodging options and looking for a Lionshead location with condo-style comfort and easy mountain access, Vail 21 Condominiums is a reliable year-round choice.

Why Guests Choose Vail 21 Condominiums

• Lionshead location near Vail Ski Resort
• Condo-style units with full kitchens
• Easy access to the Eagle Bahn Gondola
• More space for families and longer stays
• Convenient base for both winter and summer trips

Explore the stunning landscapes of the Vail Valley, search for wildlife, climb rugged mountain trails, or create your own custom off-road adventure. Whether you're seeking scenic views, hidden gems, or adrenaline-filled climbs, our expert guides ensure a safe, unforgettable journey through Colorado’s most breathtaking terrain.Book your Vail 4x4 Jeep Tour today and experience the Rockies like never before!

message.group_desc: Specialties: We are a locally owned and operated full service bike shop located in Vail Village.  Our rental fleet of bikes include everything from high-end demos to hybrid comfort bikes. Established in 2016.  Located in the heart of Vail, Colorado at the base of Gondola 1, Vail Bike Hub has been serving the Eagle County community since 2016 with a passion for all things cycling. Locally operated and deeply ingrained in the fabric of Vail's outdoor scene, owner Paul Thering has called this mountain paradise home since 2005. Drawing from years of personal exploration of the Vail area on both skis and bikes, Paul boasts an intimate knowledge of the area's trails, ensuring that every customer receives expert guidance and advice tailored to their adventure needs. Whether you're a seasoned rider or just starting out, come visit us at Vail Bike Hub for a personalized experience fueled by local expertise and a love for the great outdoors.

message.group_desc:
Tivoli Lodge is a boutique hotel offering comfortable, thoughtfully designed accommodations in a prime Vail Village location. Set along Gore Creek, the lodge combines a central village setting with a quieter, more relaxed atmosphere compared to larger resort-style hotels.

Located in Vail Village, Tivoli Lodge places guests within easy walking distance of Vail Ski Resort, village dining, shopping, and après-ski. Its creekside setting provides a peaceful feel while keeping lifts, restaurants, and seasonal events close at hand.

Rooms are well appointed and designed for comfort, making the lodge a strong option for couples and travelers who value location and a boutique experience. While the property focuses on simplicity and service rather than extensive amenities, its setting and walkability are major draws.

For travelers comparing Vail hotels and looking for a centrally located, quieter village stay with a classic alpine feel, Tivoli Lodge is a dependable choice in both winter and summer.

Why Guests Choose Tivoli Lodge

• Prime location in Vail Village along Gore Creek
• Walkable access to Vail Ski Resort, dining, and shopping
• Boutique hotel with a quieter, relaxed atmosphere
• Comfortable rooms ideal for couples and short stays
• Scenic creekside setting near village attractions

message.group_desc:
The Ritz-Carlton Club Vail is a private residence-style property located in Lionshead Village, offering direct access to skiing, dining, and year-round mountain activities. Positioned at the base of Vail Mountain near the Eagle Bahn Gondola, the property provides convenient ski-in/ski-out access during the winter season and immediate proximity to Lionshead Village amenities.

Accommodations at The Ritz-Carlton Club Vail consist of spacious multi-bedroom residences designed for extended stays and group travel. Units feature full kitchens, separate living and dining areas, and in-unit laundry, making them well-suited for families, multi-generational trips, and guests seeking the comforts of a vacation residence.

On-site amenities include an outdoor heated pool, hot tubs, fitness facilities, ski valet services, and owner-focused concierge support. The Lionshead location offers a balance of slope-side access and walkable village convenience, with easy transportation to Vail Village via pedestrian paths or shuttle service.

Why Guests Choose The Ritz-Carlton Club Vail

  • Ski-in/ski-out location at the base of Vail Mountain

  • Situated in Lionshead Village near Eagle Bahn Gondola

  • Large multi-bedroom residences with full kitchens

  • Outdoor heated pool and hot tubs year-round

  • Ideal for families, groups, and extended stays

message.group_desc:
The Sebastian Vail is a centrally located hotel in Vail Village, offering convenient access to skiing, dining, shopping, and year-round mountain activities. Situated near Gondola One, the property allows guests to reach Vail Mountain with a short walk, making it a practical choice for winter sports enthusiasts and summer visitors alike.

Accommodations at The Sebastian Vail range from traditional guest rooms to larger residences with separate living areas and kitchens, supporting both short stays and extended visits. The layout and variety of room types make the property suitable for couples, families, and small groups seeking flexibility and comfort in a village setting.

On-site amenities include an outdoor pool, hot tubs, fitness facilities, and dining options, providing guests with spaces to relax after a day on the mountain or exploring Vail. The hotel’s village location places guests within walking distance of local restaurants, boutiques, and cultural events.

Why Guests Choose The Sebastian Vail

  • Located in the heart of Vail Village near Gondola One

  • Walkable access to skiing, dining, and shopping

  • Variety of room types, including suites and residences with kitchens

  • Outdoor pool and hot tubs for year-round use

  • Suitable for couples, families, and extended stays

message.group_desc: SpecialtiesHome of Vail's original Swiss Hot Dog™, paninis, espresso, whiskey, wine, and a selection of European and craft beers.HistoryEstablished in 1967.The original Blue Cow (with an e) was built and run in Vail by Ernst and Barbara Larese after they immigrated to the United States. The Blue Cow is named after a mountain in Australia, where Ernst and Barbara used to hike. After building The Blue Cow, the Larese's opened the famed Swiss Hot Dog in Vail (1974). It was later relocated to Lionshead and more recently Avon. Simone Larese, daughter of Ernst and Barbara, has creatively combined The Swiss Hot with The Blue Cow, which is now The Blü Cow. The new location features a unique collection of bells, electric blue seating, and dark rich wood finishes. A truly unique experience of combining old with modern.Meet the Business OwnerSimone L.Business OwnerSimone is a first generation American, born from an Australian mother and Austrian (Italian/ Sud Tyrolean) father. Simone ski raced with the US Ski Team and also raced on the World Pro Tour. She has lived in Sweden, New York, Steamboat Springs, and spent "some" time in Las Vegas. She recently came back home to Vail to help her parents and to build a dream. She has literally grown up working in her parents and extended family's restaurants. She'll claim to not remember a time she never worked.
message.post_tags: Cafes, Hot Dogs, Beer, Wine & Spirits

message.group_desc: Specialties: The Fitz Bar + Bites is a local favorite in Vail for comfort food with a creative twist. Perfect for families, couples, and groups, The Fitz offers crowd-pleasing favorites like truffle fries, smashburgers, wings, and cheese curds served in a relaxed mountain setting. Join us for daily happy hour from 3 to 5 with $10 house wine, $2 off drafts and wells, and beer and shot combos. On select evenings, enjoy live music as part of the Antes de La Amp series, a pre-show activation before concerts at the Gerald R. Ford Amphitheater. Come for the food, stay for the music, and soak in the alpine energy at The Fitz. Established in 1965. Today Manor Vail is one of Vail's most iconic condominium hotels. Offering spacious condominiums, luxury penthouses, as well as a bar, restaurant, fitness center and spa.Located directly across the street from Vail Mountain's Golden Peak Ski School, chair lifts, Ski & Snowboard Club Vail, Ford Amphitheater, Ford Park and Betty Ford Alpine Gardens make Manor Vail Lodge Vail's most central hotel to all of Vail's signature events and activities.

message.group_desc:
The Galatyn Lodge is a small boutique lodge located just outside Vail Village, offering a quiet setting with convenient access to skiing, dining, and shopping. Positioned along Gore Creek and within walking distance of the Eagle Bahn Gondola, the lodge provides a balance between peaceful surroundings and easy village access.

Accommodations at The Galatyn Lodge include well-appointed guest rooms and suites, some with kitchenette-style amenities suitable for light meal preparation. The lodge’s intimate size and limited room count appeal to couples, solo travelers, and guests seeking a more relaxed, low-traffic stay compared to larger village hotels.

Amenities include an outdoor hot tub, complimentary breakfast, ski storage, and on-site parking. Its location between Vail Village and Lionshead makes it a practical option for guests who want flexibility in accessing both areas while enjoying a quieter lodging environment.

Why Guests Choose The Galatyn Lodge

  • Quiet location just outside Vail Village

  • Walkable access to Lionshead and Eagle Bahn Gondola

  • Boutique-style lodge with a small number of rooms

  • Outdoor hot tub and included breakfast

  • Well-suited for couples and short stays

message.group_desc: Specialties: Golden Bears are handcrafted in our Vail, CO studio by a team of skilled metalsmiths in several sizes & styles of pendants, rings, bracelets & earrings. The Golden Bear tradition, which began with the design of the first Mama Bear pendant, has grown into a vast line of Golden Bear jewelry and gift items. Our Golden Bears are handcrafted in our Vail studio by a team of skilled metalsmiths and are created in several sizes and styles of pendants, rings, bracelets and earrings. Precious metals such as 14k and 18k yellow and white gold, sterling silverand platinum are used to create our jewelry. Diamonds and other precious stones are often used to adorn our jewelry for added style and distinction.Our Golden Bear jewelry is displayed in our Vail Village store with the same artistry and care in which it is created. Amidst displays of our own Golden Bear designs are exquisite collections of jewelry from some of today's most exciting designers. Established in 1975.  The Golden Bear tradition, which began with the design of the first Mama Bear pendant, has grown into a vast line of Golden Bear jewelry and gift items. Our Golden Bears are handcrafted in our Vail studio by a team of skilled metalsmiths and are created in several sizes and styles of pendants, rings, bracelets and earrings. Precious metals such as 14k and 18k yellow and white gold, sterling silverand platinum are used to create our jewelry. Diamonds and other precious stones are often used to adorn our jewelry for added style and distinction.

message.group_desc: Specialties: Left Bank Restaurant serves traditional French cuisine with a modern twist in the heart of Vail Village. Using the freshest ingredients of the finest quality, chef Jean-Michel Chelain prepares every dish from scratch. Think classics like Gigolette d'Agneau and Bouillabaisse de Crustacés au Fenouil. Each item is carefully prepared and beautifully presented to offer a culinary experience unlike any other. We hope to have the pleasure of serving you not just to eat, but to dine. Established in 1970.  Celebrating its 50th anniversary, Left Bank has been serving our classic French cuisine since 1970. We strive to provide world-class service and gourmet dishes in a stunning and memorable atmosphere. With decades of dedicated success and culinary tradition, we invite you into the world of Left Bank.

message.group_desc: SpecialtiesThe best breakfast in Vail! Serving up fast, fresh, comfort food to nourish your soul. Open daily from 7 'ish' a.m. to around 2 p.m., offering Breakfast all day & Lunch after 11:30 am. Last seating is at 1:45pm. The Diner specializes in traditional favorites and regional specialties made from scratch daily using family recipes and freshest locally sourced ingredients. Come by and try the house specialty German Pancake, a sweet crepe, homemade biscuits and sausage gravy, corned beef hash, or fall in love with the Eggs Benedict the owner's father "wooed" his mother with. The list goes on.. Looking for Lunch? The Diner locally sources it's 100% all natural Angus burgers and serves them up on a freshly baked hard roll. It's all good! The "Little" part refers to the last name of the friendly owners who do all the cooking, by the way, but also to the fact that this spot isn't very big, so you may have to wait, but just "a little". Located at the west end of Lionshead in the Concert Hall Plaza building at the West Lionshead Plaza bus stop.HistoryEstablished in 2008.The Little Diner opened April 8, 2008 after an extensive renovation of the former DJ McAdams Crepes and Omelets restaurant that had occupied the space for nearly 30 years. Keeping with the tradition of the old restaurant Diner owners Brian & Peggy Little decided to keep the open kitchen and 20 seat bar design (the most efficient use of the space) as well as a few things on the menu that people had come to know and love over the years. With the remodel of the kitchen The Little Diner is now equipped to offer a full menu featuring traditional favorites, unique regional treats, and house specialties. The highly trained and experienced chefs at the diner make nearly everything on the menu from scratch daily with family recipes and take great care in preparing the freshest, highest quality, and best tasting food possible right in front of your eyes in the open kitchen. Come in and try the German Pancake, Breakfast Burrito, Biscuits and Gravy, Corned Beef Hash or Eggs Benedict!Meet the Business OwnerBrian and Peggy L.Business OwnerBrian Little was born in Memphis, TN and raised in Seattle, WA. Peggy hails from Pelham Manor, NY. Growing up Brian spent every summer and holiday in Brighton, TN at his Grandma Dot's house watching her closely in the kitchen and learning what he could. At age14 he started washing dishes in a local restaurant and worked his way up to prep cook then line cook. While attending Arizona State Brian met Peggy (they were chemistry lab partners) and they were both paying their way through school working in restaurants. When in school Brian became the Night Kitchen Manager and then Sous Chef of the bar he had worked at since moving to Tempe. Brian finished school, moved to Vail in 1998, and Peggy joined him a little later when she graduated. They both worked in the service industry in Vail over the next few years and eventually Brian was offered a Private Chef position. In late '07 the diner space came available so Brian and Peggy jumped at the opportunity and the next year they married!The Little Diner also recommendsMoe's Original BBQ263Barbeque$$Brian and Peggy L. says, “Great Bama-style BBQ! Try the specials, you won't be disappointed.”Montauk Seafood Grill281Seafood, Steakhouses, Bars$$$Brian and Peggy L. says, “Best seafood in town! Attentive and friendly staff with innovative and fresh cuisine.”Double Diamond Shoes15Ski & Snowboard Shops$$$$Brian and Peggy L. says, “Fun and knowledgeable staff with great selection, you need it they have it!”Ski Valet3Self StorageBrian and Peggy L. says, “Bike or ski trouble? Head down to Ski/ Bike Valet to see Tommy for all your skiing and cycling needs.”
message.post_location: 616 W Lionshead Cir Ste 204 Vail, CO 81657
